free_text: Investigation of activation cross-section data of
proton induced nuclear reactions on rhenium
Natural Re in the form of Mo/Re alloy
and Al and Ti monitor foils, with 36 MeV protons, 130
nA, 30 minutes at VUB, Brussels and with 70 MeV
protons, 25 nA, 25 minutes, at CYRIC, Sendai. The
high energy stack contained other target materials
too. The energy degradation in the stack was
calculated using polinomial approximation and was
checked by the monitor reactions. The activities were
determined from the photo-peak areas, taking into
account corrections for decay during irradiation,
waiting and measuring time. Decay data were taken from
The uncertainty of the beam energy in the
middle of each irradiated foil was estimated
combining the uncertainties of the energy degradation
depending on the thickness of the target foils in the
stack and the energy straggling. The uncertainty of the
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of
linearly contributing components, number of the
bombarding particles, number of the target nuclei,
decay data, detector efficiency and peak area were
summed quadratically, and the square root of the sum
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE
